title: enteropogon rupestris
intro: Enteropogon rupestris, also known as the Rock Grass or Native Grass, is a perennial grass species native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Enteropogon rupestris include its ability to soothe skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical cream, and its herbal preparations are often used to support skin health and alleviate symptoms associated with skin conditions.
